classObjective: Solve real-world optimization problems by defining variables, writing a system of inequalities, graphing, and interpreting the feasible region.
workedExamples: 7-5, Mixed Exercises — Optimization in Context: Define Variables, Write System, Graph, Find Corner Points, Optimize
warmUp: If a corner point of a feasible region is (3, 5) and the objective function is P = 4x + 3y, what is P?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: optimization in context; identifying corner points; comparing viable solutions; maximum/minimum interpretation
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice solving optimization problems using systems of inequalities and corner point evaluation
independentPractice: Practice problems solving real-world optimization problems using systems of inequalities
assessment: Exit ticket: solve an optimization problem: maximize profit given production time and material constraints
capReflection: Why are the maximum and minimum values of an optimization problem found at corner points of the feasible region?
